description: Zingiberaceae is a family of aromatic monocotyledonous herbs comprising about 50 genera and over 1,600 species, distributed across tropical and subtropical Asia, Africa, and the Americas. The family is enormously important economically: ginger, turmeric, and cardamom — three of India's most essential spices — all belong to Zingiberaceae. Many genera also produce spectacular ornamental flowers and are foundational to Indian Ayurvedic medicine.
meta_description: Shop Zingiberaceae — ginger, turmeric, cardamom, alpinia, and hedychium. Spice plants, medicinal rhizomes, and ornamental gingers with Indian common names.
meta_keywords: Zingiberaceae, ginger plant, turmeric plant, cardamom plant, Adrak, Haldi, Elaichi, ornamental ginger India

genera.description: Zingiber is a genus of about 100–150 species of rhizomatous herbs native to tropical Asia, with the majority of species occurring in Southeast Asia. The genus includes Z. officinale, one of the most important spice plants in the world and a cornerstone of Indian cooking and Ayurveda. Plants arise from aromatic underground rhizomes and bear pseudostems with lanceolate leaves. Several species produce ornamental inflorescences.
genera.meta_description: Buy Zingiber species — culinary ginger, ornamental gingers, and rare wild species. Rhizome sets and potted plants with full cultivation notes.
genera.meta_keywords: Zingiber officinale, ginger plant for sale, Adrak plant, culinary ginger, ornamental ginger India
genera.native_range: Tropical Asia, primarily Southeast Asia and Indian Subcontinent

species.description: Zingiber officinale is one of the world's most important spice crops, cultivated throughout tropical India. The aromatic rhizome is used fresh (Adrak / green ginger), dried (Sonth / Saunth), and as a powdered spice. It is a foundational ingredient in Indian cooking, chai, and Ayurvedic medicine where it is known as 'Vishwabheshaj' (universal medicine). India is the world's largest producer of ginger.
species.meta_description: Buy ginger rhizome sets and plants. Zingiber officinale — India's essential spice; suitable for pots, kitchen gardens, and farm-scale cultivation.
species.meta_keywords: ginger plant India, Zingiber officinale, Adrak plant, ginger rhizome, culinary ginger buy
species.native_range: India and Southeast Asia (likely domesticated in India or South Asia)
species.notes: Dormant rhizomes planted at onset of monsoon. Requires well-drained, fertile loamy soil. Major varieties: Rio-de-Janeiro, Maran, Nadia, Suprabha, Suruchi, Suravi.

species.common_name: Shampoo Ginger / Pinecone Ginger / Zerumbet / Kachur (कचूर)
species.description: Zingiber zerumbet is a widespread wild ginger species found across India, Southeast Asia, and the Pacific. It produces dramatic pinecone-shaped inflorescences that turn from green to bright red when mature; the bracts exude a soapy, fragrant liquid used traditionally as a shampoo and hair conditioner. The rhizome is used medicinally in Ayurveda.
species.meta_title: Zingiber zerumbet — Shampoo Ginger / Pinecone Ginger
species.meta_description: Buy Zingiber zerumbet, the shampoo ginger with dramatic red pinecone inflorescences. Ornamental and medicinal; easy tropical garden plant.
species.meta_keywords: Zingiber zerumbet, shampoo ginger, pinecone ginger, ornamental ginger India, Kachur plant
species.notes: Ornamental and medicinal. Inflorescences used as natural shampoo. Very easy to grow in tropical and subtropical India.

genera.description: Curcuma is a genus of about 100 species native to tropical Asia, with India being the primary centre of diversity and cultivation. C. longa — turmeric — is India's most iconic spice and medicinal plant, with the rhizome yielding curcumin, a celebrated anti-inflammatory compound. Several other Curcuma species produce stunning ornamental inflorescences and are important components of Indian floriculture.
genera.meta_description: Buy Curcuma species — turmeric rhizomes, medicinal Curcuma, and spectacular ornamental varieties. Indian common names and cultivation notes included.
genera.meta_keywords: Curcuma longa, turmeric plant, Haldi plant, ornamental Curcuma, Siam tulip India, buy turmeric rhizome
genera.native_range: South Asia and Southeast Asia, with centre of diversity in India

species.description: Curcuma longa is arguably India's most culturally significant spice plant. The bright orange-yellow rhizome is a staple in Indian cooking, religious ceremonies (auspicious Haldi rituals in Hindu weddings), and Ayurvedic and Unani medicine. India produces over 75% of the world's turmeric. Telangana, Andhra Pradesh, Orissa, Tamil Nadu, Karnataka, and Maharashtra are major producing states. Active compound curcumin has extensive documented anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
species.meta_description: Buy turmeric rhizomes and plants. Curcuma longa — India's golden spice and medicine; kitchen garden and farm cultivation notes included.
species.meta_keywords: turmeric plant India, Curcuma longa, Haldi rhizome, buy turmeric plant, curcumin plant
species.native_range: India (likely originated in South Asia; naturalised pantropical)
species.notes: Major Indian varieties: Alleppey Finger, Madras, Rajapore, Salem, Erode. High curcumin varieties available for nutraceutical cultivation.

species.common_name: White Turmeric / Zedoary / Amb Halad (आंब हळद) / Kachur
species.description: Curcuma zedoaria is a medicinal and culinary species native to Northeast India and Southeast Asia. The white-fleshed rhizome has a distinct camphor-like fragrance and is used in Ayurvedic preparations, traditional South and Southeast Asian cooking, and as a digestive. Also known as white turmeric in Indian markets.
species.meta_title: Curcuma zedoaria — White Turmeric / Amb Halad
species.meta_description: Buy Curcuma zedoaria, white turmeric and Ayurvedic medicinal rhizome. Known as Amb Halad and Kachur in Indian medicine.
species.meta_keywords: Curcuma zedoaria, white turmeric, zedoary, Amb Halad, Ayurvedic plant India
species.notes: Used in Ayurveda and traditional cooking. Attractive ornamental foliage with purple stripe on leaves.

species.description: Curcuma alismatifolia is a spectacular ornamental species native to Thailand, Laos, and Cambodia. It produces elegant, tulip-like inflorescences in shades of white, pink, and purple. Though not native to India, it has become a popular cut flower and potted flowering plant in the Indian horticultural market, especially in urban plant retail.
species.meta_description: Buy Curcuma alismatifolia, the Siam tulip with gorgeous pink or white blooms. Popular ornamental ginger for pots and cut flower use in India.
species.meta_keywords: Curcuma alismatifolia, Siam tulip, ornamental Curcuma, Thailand tulip, flowering ginger India
species.notes: Excellent cut flower and pot plant. Goes dormant in winter; store rhizomes dry. Prefers partial shade in hot Indian summers.

genera.description: Elettaria is a small genus of rhizomatous herbs in the ginger family. The genus is best known for E. cardamomum — true (green) cardamom — the world's third most expensive spice after saffron and vanilla, and a spice of profound importance to Indian cuisine, chai, and Ayurveda. India (particularly Kerala and Karnataka) and Guatemala are the primary producing countries.
genera.meta_description: Buy Elettaria cardamomum — green cardamom plants. India's premium spice; shade-loving forest herb from the Western Ghats. Full cultivation guide.
genera.meta_keywords: Elettaria cardamomum, cardamom plant India, Elaichi plant, green cardamom buy, Western Ghats spice plant
genera.native_range: Western Ghats of India (Kerala, Karnataka) and Sri Lanka

species.common_name: Green Cardamom / Elaichi (इलायची) / Elakkai (ஏலக்காய்) / Elachi / Choti Elaichi
species.description: Elettaria cardamomum is the queen of Indian spices — a shade-loving perennial herb native to the moist evergreen forests of India's Western Ghats. The aromatic seed pods are indispensable in Indian cooking (biryanis, sweets, masalas), chai, and pan-South Asian culinary traditions. It is also a key ingredient in Ayurvedic formulations and traditional medicine. Kerala's Idukki district is the heartland of Indian cardamom cultivation.
species.meta_description: Buy cardamom plants — Elettaria cardamomum. Western Ghats' prized spice; shade-loving plantation crop with TC planting material available.
species.meta_keywords: cardamom plant, Elettaria cardamomum, Elaichi plant India, green cardamom cultivation, buy cardamom seedling
species.native_range: Western Ghats of Kerala and Karnataka, India; Sri Lanka
species.notes: Requires cool, shaded, humid conditions with well-drained acidic soil. Major varieties: Malabar, Mysore, Vazhukka. Kerala's Idukki district is the prime cultivation zone.

genera.description: Alpinia is one of the largest genera in the ginger family, comprising about 230 species distributed across tropical and subtropical Asia and the Pacific. The genus includes important medicinal species (galangal), aromatic culinary herbs, and spectacular ornamental garden plants. Several species are native to India's northeastern states and the Western Ghats.
genera.meta_description: Buy Alpinia species — galangal, shell ginger, and ornamental alpinias. Culinary, medicinal, and landscape varieties with Indian common names.
genera.meta_keywords: Alpinia galanga, galangal plant, Kulanjan, shell ginger India, Alpinia purpurata, ornamental ginger
genera.native_range: Tropical Asia and Pacific; centre of diversity in Southeast Asia

species.common_name: Greater Galangal / Kulanjan (कुलंजन) / Sugandha Vacha / Thai Ginger
species.description: Alpinia galanga is a rhizomatous herb native to Southeast Asia and cultivated across tropical India. The pungent, peppery rhizome is used in South Indian and coastal cuisines, Ayurvedic medicine, and widely in Thai and Indonesian cooking. In India, it is known as Kulanjan and used in digestive tonics and anti-inflammatory preparations. The plant grows vigorously in tropical conditions.
species.meta_description: Buy Alpinia galanga — greater galangal rhizomes and plants. Used in South Indian, Ayurvedic, and Southeast Asian cuisine and medicine.
species.meta_keywords: Alpinia galanga, galangal India, Kulanjan plant, Thai ginger, greater galangal buy
species.native_range: Southeast Asia; cultivated across tropical India
species.notes: Vigorous grower; tolerates partial shade. Rhizomes used fresh or dried in cooking and medicine.

species.common_name: Red Ginger / Pink Ginger / Lal Adrak
species.description: Alpinia purpurata is a tall ornamental ginger from the Pacific Islands, widely grown across tropical India as a landscape plant and cut flower. It produces tall, showy inflorescences with brilliant red or pink bracts that are long-lasting as cut flowers. A common sight in Kerala and Goa hotel gardens and tropical landscaping.
species.meta_description: Buy Alpinia purpurata, the red ginger with spectacular long-lasting red or pink inflorescences. Popular tropical landscape and cut flower plant.
species.meta_keywords: Alpinia purpurata, red ginger India, ornamental ginger, tropical cut flower, pink ginger plant buy
species.native_range: New Guinea and Pacific Islands; widely cultivated in tropics
species.notes: Excellent cut flower; inflorescences last 2–3 weeks. Prefers full sun to partial shade with ample water. Very popular in Kerala and coastal India.

species.common_name: Shell Ginger / Pink Porcelain Lily / Variegated Shell Ginger
species.description: Alpinia zerumbet is a large, vigorous ornamental ginger species widely cultivated in tropical India. It is best known in cultivation through its 'Variegata' cultivar, with striking yellow-and-green variegated foliage. The pendulous inflorescences bear white and pink shell-like flowers with yellow-red-streaked lips. Both aromatic and ornamental.
species.meta_description: Buy Alpinia zerumbet shell ginger — stunning variegated foliage and fragrant shell-like flowers. Popular ornamental for tropical Indian gardens.
species.meta_keywords: Alpinia zerumbet, shell ginger, variegated ginger India, pink porcelain lily, ornamental Alpinia
species.native_range: East Asia and Southeast Asia; widely naturalised in tropics
species.notes: 'Variegata' is the most popular cultivar — striking yellow-and-green striped leaves. Excellent for mixed tropical borders.

cultivars.name: Variegata
cultivars.description: Bold yellow-and-green variegated foliage form. Most widely sold Alpinia zerumbet in Indian nurseries. Slightly less vigorous than the green species but more ornamentally valued.
cultivars.notes: Best colour in partial shade; full sun may scorch variegation. Very popular in South Indian hotel and resort landscaping.

genera.description: Hedychium is a genus of about 80 species of rhizomatous herbs native primarily to the Himalayan foothills, Northeast India, and Southeast Asia. Known as ginger lilies or butterfly gingers, they produce some of the most fragrant and spectacular flowers in the ginger family. H. coronarium is the national flower of Cuba and is sacred in Hindu ritual in parts of India, where it is used in temple offerings and the essential oil in perfumery.
genera.meta_description: Buy Hedychium species — fragrant ginger lilies from the Himalayas. White, yellow, orange, and red flowered species for tropical and subtropical gardens.
genera.meta_keywords: Hedychium, ginger lily India, Sontakka plant, butterfly ginger, fragrant Hedychium buy, Himalayan ginger
genera.native_range: Himalayan foothills, Northeast India, Southeast Asia, southern China

species.common_name: White Ginger Lily / Sontakka (सोनटक्का) / Gulbakauli / Dolan Champa
species.description: Hedychium coronarium is the most widely cultivated ginger lily, prized for its intensely fragrant white butterfly-like flowers. It is native to the Himalayan foothills of India and Nepal and is widely naturalised across tropical Asia and the Americas. In India, the flowers are used in temple offerings (Dolan Champa in Bengal), garlands, and the essential oil is an ingredient in Indian perfumery. The national flower of Cuba.
species.meta_title: Hedychium coronarium — White Ginger Lily / Sontakka
species.meta_description: Buy Hedychium coronarium, the intensely fragrant white ginger lily. Sacred in Indian temples; beautiful garden and container plant.
species.meta_keywords: Hedychium coronarium, white ginger lily, Sontakka plant, butterfly ginger, fragrant garden plant India
species.native_range: Himalayan foothills of India and Nepal; widely naturalised
species.notes: Extremely fragrant flowers, especially in evenings. Prefers moist, rich soil and partial shade. Naturalises readily in tropical India.

species.description: Hedychium gardnerianum is a tall, robust ginger lily native to the Eastern Himalayas and Northeast India. It produces large, showy inflorescences of fragrant yellow flowers with long red stamens. While valued as an ornamental in India, it has become an invasive species in New Zealand, Azores, and Hawaii. One of the most spectacular Hedychium species for large tropical gardens.
species.meta_description: Buy Hedychium gardnerianum, the Kahili ginger with stunning yellow and red flower spikes. Native to the Eastern Himalayas.
species.meta_keywords: Hedychium gardnerianum, Kahili ginger, Indian ginger lily, yellow ginger lily, Eastern Himalaya plant
species.native_range: Eastern Himalayas, Sikkim, Northeast India, Bhutan
species.notes: Large spectacular plant to 2 m. Fragrant yellow flowers with red filaments. Excellent for large garden borders and accent planting.

species.common_name: Scarlet Ginger Lily / Orange Butterfly Ginger
species.description: Hedychium coccineum is a striking ginger lily native to the Himalayan foothills of India, Nepal, and Bhutan, producing dense spikes of brilliant orange to red flowers. One of the most vividly coloured Hedychium species, it is popular in Indian hill station gardens and increasingly in tropical landscaping projects.
species.meta_description: Buy Hedychium coccineum, the scarlet ginger lily with vivid orange-red flower spikes. Native to the Indian Himalayas.
species.meta_keywords: Hedychium coccineum, scarlet ginger lily, orange ginger lily, Himalayan ginger lily, ornamental ginger India
species.native_range: Himalayan foothills of India, Nepal, Bhutan; northeast India
species.notes: Bold orange-red flowers. Prefers cool, humid conditions; excellent for Himalayan foothills gardens (Darjeeling, Mussoorie, Ooty).
